reviews.review_msg: Excellent prof. Pay attention, do the readings, don't be lazy, and the assignments actually get interesting. It is obvious that Jonathon has high interest in what he teaches, which is what makes the lectures interesting.
great teacher; i hate poetry. the students in this class think that poetry means easy reading and get upset when they realize that they actually have to work.

Okay, this guy is not a prof, he's a fan. Obviously he is really into poetry (which kind of makes the class have interest) but the things he goes into detail about are THE MOST IRRELEVANT. Any knowledge I had about the poems was from the tutorial instructor (great guy). The class was not worth it & should have been called Poetry&Internet Relations.

Doesn't post up notes, so lots of writing notes during lecture. The free verse poems were...difficult. The assignments were doable. The prof is knowledgeable about his subject, but sometimes he leaves you wondering what exactly you learned. The final exam was not bad at all, just practice off the practice exam.

I've always hated English with a passion, and this prof makes me hate it more. Lectures were awfully boring, free verse poems were utter trash. The final was unexpectedly doable, and the workload was very good. But this prof does nothing to make the class engaging. Make sure you take lots of notes in class, b/c he posts barely anything.

lol the comment below. honestly, this course is good if u can BS ur points with justification while memorizing key poetry terms. final was WAY TOO LONG, too much to write. sometimes felt as if poems made no sense. mixed feelings about this class tbh

WHAT WAS THAT?? The final was horrible... Maybe instead of wasting 1 week on useless/unrelated guest poets he should have had review, what a joke this guy shouldn't been even teaching students at a university level yet alone at a elementary level.

Though hes a good speaker and fun to listen to, He rambles and makes you not totally sure what you learnt. Showed interesting videos and websites which made poetry inspiring. Not enough time during the final. Felt unprepared. Practice exam = almost exactly the final. Learn Scansion and all poem meanings on your own before the exam.

The sonnets that he interpreted at the beginning of the term were really interesting. After that, it was all free verse poems, like TOO FREE to the point where random words were placed together and contained absolutely no meaning. I was thinking "Even I could write stuff like this and get it published." TAs often sexually interpreted poems.

His lectures are extremely boring and dry. He doesn't post up lecture slides or recordings so if you miss class then you get nothing. You will think, "why am I even here, how will this class EVER be USEFUL?" It won't. The course is all about over analyzing the literary devices and syllables in poems.

He's a really good speaker and seems like he knows what he's saying. However he can go on and on and generally the lectures can turn out to be very dry. I always see kids sleeping beside me. Pretty easy though work load wise, only 3 Assignments and then a Final
hes a good prof but a hard marker, the final should have been 3 hours instead of 2. too much to do in way too little time, and make sure to use MLA format properly on your essays he deducts marks
